sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content:
History of autoimmune disorders: Patients with SPTCL often have a personal or family history of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) or other autoimmune disorders.
Germline HAVCR2 mutations: Germline mutations in the HAVCR2 gene (encoding TIM-3) are found in 25%-85% of SPTCL cases and are associated with hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) and a more severe disease course. They are more prevalent in patients of Asian ancestry.
HTLV-1 infection: HTLV-1 serology is recommended to exclude adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma (ATLL), which can mimic cutaneous lymphomas. ATLL is associated with HTLV-1 infection.
Immunosuppression: Patients on immunosuppressive therapy are at increased risk for UV radiation-associated skin malignancies, which is a consideration when using phototherapy for cutaneous lymphomas.
Age: MF and SS can occur at any age but are more common in older adults. Sézary syndrome predominantly affects older individuals. For PCDLBCL, leg type, age is a factor in treatment selection.
Drug exposure (Lymphomatoid drug reactions): Lymphomatoid drug reactions, which can mimic CD30+ T-cell LPDs, have been linked to drugs such as amlodipine, carbamazepine, cefuroxime, and valsartan.

cards.strength: Strong association mentioned in context of SPTCL
cards.detail: Patients with SPTCL often have a personal or family history of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) or other autoimmune disorders.

cards.strength: Strong predisposing factor for SPTCL with HLH
cards.detail: Germline mutations in the HAVCR2 gene (encoding TIM-3) are found in 25%-85% of SPTCL cases and are associated with hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) and a more severe disease course. They are more prevalent in patients of Asian ancestry.

cards.strength: Causal for ATLL; relevant differential for cutaneous lymphomas
cards.detail: HTLV-1 serology is recommended to exclude adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma (ATLL), which can mimic cutaneous lymphomas. ATLL is associated with HTLV-1 infection.

cards.strength: Modifying factor for secondary malignancy risk during treatment
cards.detail: Patients on immunosuppressive therapy are at increased risk for UV radiation-associated skin malignancies, which is a consideration when using phototherapy for cutaneous lymphomas.
MF and SS can occur at any age but are more common in older adults. Sézary syndrome predominantly affects older individuals. For PCDLBCL, leg type, age is a factor in treatment selection.

cards.strength: Known association for a differential diagnosis
cards.detail: Lymphomatoid drug reactions, which can mimic CD30+ T-cell LPDs, have been linked to drugs such as amlodipine, carbamazepine, cefuroxime, and valsartan.
